extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. Rule r2 (priority 50): IF prior_gi_bleed THEN rec_drug_a := true. Rule r4 (priority 50): IF prior_gi_bleed THEN rec_drug_b := true. Both rules are triggered by the same single condition prior_gi_bleed. r2 is the only rule affecting rec_drug_a and r4 is the only rule affecting rec_drug_b, so there are no competing rules or defaults that could set them back to false once prior_gi_bleed is true (defaults apply only to outputs left unset). Therefore any input with prior_gi_bleed = true forces rec_drug_a = true and rec_drug_b = true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. Concrete counterexample: prior_gi_bleed = true, all other inputs false. Trace: r2 fires -> rec_drug_a = true; r4 fires -> rec_drug_b = true; r1/r3/r5 not triggered so rec_drug_c defaults to false. Result rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b = true, violating the property.
